hypotheses.title: ECM Stiffness Nucleates Mechanosensitive Super-Enhancers via YAP-Dependent BRD4/MED1 Phase Condensate Assembly

connection.bridge: YAP/TEAD-recruited EP300 seeds H3K27ac → BRD4 binding exceeds phase separation threshold → super-enhancer condensate assembly

hypotheses.confidence_justification: Individual components well-supported (YAP nuclear translocation, EP300 coactivator, BRD4 phase separation, EP300-BRD4 STRING 0.988). Novel claim is their coordination under ECM stiffness to drive super-enhancer assembly.
hypotheses.groundedness_explanation: YAP-TEAD-EP300 (STRING 0.692), EP300-BRD4 (STRING 0.988), BRD4 at super-enhancers are GROUNDED. ECM stiffness → super-enhancer assembly chain is PARAMETRIC. BRD4 phase separation threshold in vivo is PARAMETRIC.
grounded_claims: YAP nuclear translocation on stiff substrates (Dupont Nature 2011)
EP300-BRD4 interaction (STRING 0.988)
YAP-BRD4 co-occurrence (31 PubMed papers)
ECM stiffness + H3K27ac = 0 papers (computational validation)
parametric_claims: BRD4 phase separation threshold (~5-10 µM) applied to in vivo enhancer context
EP300 kcat ~5/min extrapolation to in vivo
Sustained YAP occupancy specifically crosses condensate nucleation threshold
Mechano-super-enhancers as a distinct category
hypotheses.why_wrong: YAP-TEAD may not recruit EP300 at sufficient density to cross BRD4 phase separation threshold without co-signals. Super-enhancer formation may require SWI/SNF remodeling. BRD4 condensates may be disrupted by mechanical forces themselves. EP300-YAP STRING score (0.692) is medium confidence.
hypotheses.literature_gap: Gap #2 (super-enhancer mechanosensitivity under ECM stiffness) and Gap #4 (phase-separated condensates at enhancers as mechanical sensors). Also: ECM stiffness + H3K27ac = 0 papers absolute disjunction.
hypotheses.falsifiable_prediction: H3K27ac + BRD4 ChIP-seq on cells at 1/5/10/50 kPa will reveal stiffness-dependent super-enhancer gain enriched for TEAD motifs. JQ1 should collapse mechano-super-enhancers.
hypotheses.test_protocol: MCF10A on PA hydrogels (1/5/10/50 kPa, 24h). H3K27ac ChIP-seq + BRD4 ChIP-seq + ATAC-seq + RNA-seq. ROSE algorithm super-enhancer calling. TEAD motif analysis. JQ1 500nM validation.

hypotheses.title: LINC-Transmitted Mechanical Force Detaches Enhancers from Lamina-Associated Domains, Creating Stiffness-Dependent Enhancer Derepression

connection.bridge: LINC complex force transmission → physical detachment of LAD-sequestered enhancers from nuclear lamina
connection.field_c: Enhancer derepression at facultative LAD boundaries (epigenomics)

hypotheses.confidence_justification: LADs well-characterized, LINC force transmission verified (STRING 0.999), fLAD variability established. But physical force detachment of fLAD boundaries is untested. Force distribution per chromatin contact may be insufficient.
hypotheses.groundedness_explanation: LINC complex (STRING 0.999), LADs (Guelen Nature 2008), fLAD/cLAD distinction (Meuleman Genome Research 2013), lamin A/C upregulation by stiffness (Xu 2023, Mandal 2025) are GROUNDED. Mechanical detachment mechanism and force-conductor model for lamin A/C are PARAMETRIC.
grounded_claims: LADs sequester ~35-40% of genome at nuclear periphery (Guelen Nature 2008)
LINC complex: SUN2-LMNA STRING 0.999
LAP2β transmits force from lamina to chromatin (Sun 2023)
ECM stiffness upregulates lamin A/C (Xu 2023, Mandal 2025)
fLAD vs cLAD distinction (Meuleman Genome Research 2013)
parametric_claims: Physical detachment of fLAD boundaries by LINC-transmitted force
Lamin A/C as rigid force conductor (not compliant damper) on stiff ECM
Directional/anisotropic enhancer derepression based on stress fiber axis
30 pN distributed across ~100k contacts = ~0.3 pN per contact (may be insufficient)
hypotheses.why_wrong: LAD tethering may be primarily biochemical (HP1-H3K9me2/3), not mechanical. 30 pN distributed across 100k+ contacts yields only ~0.3 pN per contact — insufficient for detachment. Increased lamin A/C may strengthen LAD tethering, opposing the mechanism.
hypotheses.literature_gap: Gap #5 (LAD vs non-LAD enhancer mechanosensitivity). Also addresses Sun 2020 observation that nuclear periphery genes resist force-induced activation.
hypotheses.falsifiable_prediction: DamID-seq + H3K27ac ChIP-seq on 1/10/50 kPa: stiffness-dependent fLAD boundary loss + H3K27ac gain at detached regions. LAP2β and lamin A/C knockdown should abolish/reverse the effect.
hypotheses.test_protocol: C2C12 myoblasts on PA hydrogels (1/10/50 kPa, 48h). DamID-seq (lamin B1-Dam) + H3K27ac ChIP-seq + ATAC-seq + RNA-seq. Map fLAD boundary shifts. siLAP2β and siLMNA perturbations.

hypotheses.title: ECM Stiffness Coordinates a Dual-Enzyme Bivalent-to-Active Enhancer Switch via Concurrent KDM6B Activation and YAP-EP300 Recruitment

connection.bridge: Simultaneous KDM6B (H3K27me3 demethylase) upregulation + YAP-TEAD-EP300 (H3K27ac writer) recruitment → coordinated H3K27me3→H3K27ac switch at bivalent enhancers
connection.field_c: Bivalent-to-active enhancer conversion at developmental enhancers (epigenomics)

hypotheses.confidence_justification: Strongest of the set — both enzymes independently verified as mechanosensitive (KDM6B by Yu 2025, EP300 via YAP-TEAD). H3K27me3/H3K27ac mutual exclusivity is biochemical fact. Novel claim is coordinated operation at same enhancers.
hypotheses.groundedness_explanation: KDM6B upregulation by ECM stiffness (Yu MCB 2025, ChIP-qPCR), EP300 for mechanoresponsive transcription (Whitworth 2024), H3K27me3/H3K27ac mutual exclusivity (canonical), poised enhancers (Rada-Iglesias Nature 2011) are GROUNDED. Coordinated operation at same loci is PARAMETRIC.
grounded_claims: KDM6B demethylates H3K27me3 (canonical enzymology)
ECM stiffness upregulates KDM6B expression and activity (Yu MCB 2025)
EP300 writes H3K27ac at enhancers (canonical)
H3K27me3 and H3K27ac mutually exclusive on same K27 residue (canonical biochemistry)
YAP-TEAD recruits EP300 (STRING 0.692)
parametric_claims: KDM6B and EP300 operate at the SAME enhancer loci under ECM stiffness
KDM6B activation is YAP-independent (distinct mechanosensitive arm)
The dual-enzyme mechanism is unique to ECM stiffness among mechanical stimuli
GSK-J4 + A-485 combined effect exceeds individual inhibition
hypotheses.why_wrong: KDM6B targets (Snail/Twist promoters per Yu 2025) may not overlap with YAP-TEAD-EP300 targets (CTGF/CYR61 enhancers). KDM6B mechanosensitivity shown in thyroid cancer cells may not generalize. SWI/SNF remodeling may be an additional required step. EP300 writing rate may lose kinetic race against PRC2 re-methylation.
hypotheses.literature_gap: Anomaly #3 (H3K27me3/H3K27ac antagonism under mechanical regulation — coordinated switch at enhancers not studied). Also explains ECM stiffness-driven stem cell differentiation at enhancer level.
hypotheses.falsifiable_prediction: CUT&Tag for H3K27me3 + H3K27ac on 1/5/10/50 kPa: same enhancers lose H3K27me3 and gain H3K27ac. GSK-J4 blocks H3K27me3 loss; A-485 blocks H3K27ac gain; combined treatment abolishes the switch completely.
hypotheses.test_protocol: hMSCs on PA hydrogels (1/10/50 kPa, 72h). CUT&Tag H3K27me3 + H3K27ac. GSK-J4 (10µM) + A-485 (3µM) single and combined perturbations on 50 kPa. ATAC-seq + RNA-seq. Overlap with known poised enhancer maps.

hypotheses.title: Stiffness-Induced Enhancer H3K27ac Creates Self-Sustaining Transcriptional Condensates That Persist as Mechanical Memory

connection.bridge: H3K27ac → BRD4 condensate → eRNA → condensate reinforcement → H3K27ac self-maintenance → persistence after force removal

hypotheses.confidence_justification: Positive feedback concept mechanistically logical. Each component individually supported. Novel claim: this specific feedback loop IS the mechanism of mechanical memory at enhancers. Yang 2014 demonstrated mechanical memory but not at enhancer resolution.
hypotheses.groundedness_explanation: Mechanical memory concept (Hsia JMB 2023, Yang 2014), H3K27ac→BRD4→P-TEFb cascade (canonical), eRNA from active enhancers (canonical) are GROUNDED. eRNA stabilization of condensates (emerging) and specific feedback loop as memory mechanism are PARAMETRIC.
grounded_claims: Mechanical memory in MSCs (Yang et al. 2014)
Mechanical memory stored as epigenetic modifications (Hsia JMB 2023)
H3K27ac recruits BRD4 via bromodomains (canonical)
BRD4 recruits P-TEFb for transcription elongation (canonical)
eRNAs transcribed from active enhancers (canonical)
parametric_claims: eRNAs stabilize BRD4/MED1 phase-separated condensates sufficiently for self-maintenance
Condensate-associated EP300 writes H3K27ac at sufficient rate to overcome HDAC erasure
Feedback loop establishes within 48-72h (shorter than Yang 2014's >10 day memory)
Decay kinetics follow condensate stability, not single-molecule dynamics
hypotheses.why_wrong: HDACs continuously remove H3K27ac — without YAP-EP300 recruitment, erasing rate may exceed writing rate. eRNAs are extremely unstable (half-life minutes). Memory may be encoded in DNA methylation or TAD architecture instead. Yang 2014 showed >10 day exposure needed — enhancer-level memory establishing faster would be inconsistent.
hypotheses.literature_gap: Gap #7 (mechanical memory at enhancers: proposed but untested at enhancer resolution). Also addresses Hsia 2023 by providing specific molecular mechanism for epigenetic memory.
hypotheses.falsifiable_prediction: Cells on 50 kPa for 7d → transfer to 1 kPa: subset of 'memory enhancers' retain H3K27ac, produce eRNAs, show BRD4 occupancy. JQ1 within 24h of transfer erases memory. <24h stiffness exposure = no memory establishment.
hypotheses.test_protocol: hMSCs on 50 kPa (7d) → 1 kPa (0/3/7/14d). H3K27ac ChIP-seq + BRD4 ChIP-seq + GRO-seq + YAP IF at each timepoint. JQ1/A-485 applied at transfer. Actinomycin D (100nM) as eRNA-dependent memory test.

hypotheses.title: ECM Stiffness Rewires Enhancer-Promoter 3D Chromatin Loops via YAP-TEAD-Dependent NIPBL Recruitment and Cohesin Redistribution

connection.bridge: YAP-TEAD → EP300 → H3K27ac → BRD4 → NIPBL (cohesin loader) recruitment → cohesin redistribution → new enhancer-promoter loops

hypotheses.confidence_justification: Each step individually supported but the full 6-step chain has many potential failure points. No Hi-C under ECM stiffness exists. BRD4-NIPBL interaction published but not universally reproduced.
hypotheses.groundedness_explanation: NIPBL at enhancers (Kagey Nature 2010), cohesin loop extrusion (canonical), YAP-TEAD-EP300 chain (STRING 0.692) are GROUNDED. BRD4-NIPBL interaction (Olley Nature Genetics 2018) is PARTIALLY GROUNDED. ECM stiffness → cohesin redistribution chain is PARAMETRIC.
grounded_claims: NIPBL recruited to active enhancers with Mediator (Kagey Nature 2010)
Cohesin-mediated loop extrusion blocked by CTCF (canonical)
H3K27ac read by BRD4 (canonical)
BRD4-NIPBL interaction (Olley Nature Genetics 2018)
parametric_claims: ECM stiffness drives cohesin redistribution via the YAP→H3K27ac→BRD4→NIPBL chain
New enhancer-promoter loops form that did not exist on soft substrates
Stiffness-dependent loops are functionally distinct from constitutive CTCF loops
BRD4-NIPBL mechanism is a general feature (not specific to Cornelia de Lange context)
hypotheses.why_wrong: Loops may be primarily CTCF-determined (genetically encoded) with little enhancer-driven loading. BRD4-NIPBL may be context-specific. Hi-C resolution may be insufficient to detect individual enhancer-promoter loops. The 6-step cascade attenuates signal at each step.
hypotheses.literature_gap: Gap #3 (3D genome topology / Hi-C under ECM stiffness gradients: unstudied). Addresses whether ECM stiffness rewires gene regulatory circuits at the architectural level.
hypotheses.falsifiable_prediction: H3K27ac HiChIP on 1/10/50 kPa: stiffness-dependent enhancer-promoter loops anchored at TEAD-motif enhancers. siNIPBL and JQ1 should abolish mechano-loops. Verteporfin should abolish mechano-loops.
hypotheses.test_protocol: MCF10A on PA hydrogels (1/10/50 kPa, 48h). H3K27ac HiChIP. Differential loop calling. TEAD motif enrichment at mechano-loop anchors. siNIPBL, JQ1, verteporfin perturbations. 4C-seq validation.

hypotheses.title: Tissue-Specific ECM Stiffness Selects Tissue-Specific Enhancer Programs Through Differential YAP Nuclear Residence Time and EP300 Occupancy Kinetics

connection.bridge: YAP nuclear residence time as quantitative rheostat → EP300 occupancy kinetics → threshold-dependent enhancer cohort activation

hypotheses.confidence_justification: Graded YAP nuclear translocation is well-established (Elosegui-Artola Cell 2017). Tissue-specific stiffness values are known. The kinetic selection model is logical but untested. Comparison to native tissue enhancer profiles is a strong validation strategy.
hypotheses.groundedness_explanation: Graded YAP mechanosensing (Dupont 2011, Elosegui-Artola Cell 2017), tissue stiffness values (Engler Cell 2006), EP300 as YAP-TEAD coactivator (STRING 0.692) are GROUNDED. Kinetic selection model and stiffness-as-morphogen analogy are PARAMETRIC.
grounded_claims: Graded YAP nuclear/cytoplasmic ratio vs stiffness (Elosegui-Artola Cell 2017)
Tissue-specific ECM stiffness values (Engler Cell 2006)
YAP-TEAD recruits EP300 (STRING 0.692)
MSC differentiation directed by substrate stiffness (Engler Cell 2006)
parametric_claims: EP300 residence time at enhancers determines H3K27ac probability
TEAD motif affinity correlates with enhancer activation threshold
Hierarchical (not distinct) enhancer activation with increasing stiffness
Stiffness-dependent enhancer profiles recapitulate native tissue profiles
Stiffness functions as a mechanical morphogen for enhancer selection
hypotheses.why_wrong: Tissue-specific enhancers are primarily determined by lineage-specific TFs (MYOD, RUNX2, etc.), not stiffness alone. EP300 may not be limiting — other coactivators may determine activation. Enhancer activation may be cooperative/switch-like rather than graded. In vivo complexity (stiffness + topography + chemistry) may overwhelm the isolated stiffness effect.
hypotheses.literature_gap: Gap #6 (tissue-specific ECM stiffness → tissue-specific enhancer programs: unexplored). Provides enhancer-level mechanism for Engler 2006 classic stiffness-directed MSC differentiation.
hypotheses.falsifiable_prediction: hMSCs on 0.5/2/8/20/50 kPa: progressively more active enhancers. Low-threshold enhancers (2 kPa) subset of high-stiffness enhancers (hierarchical). Low-threshold enhancers have higher-affinity TEAD motifs. Enhancer profiles at each stiffness resemble native tissue profiles.
hypotheses.test_protocol: hMSCs on PA hydrogels (0.5/2/8/20/50 kPa, 72h). H3K27ac ChIP-seq + ATAC-seq + RNA-seq. Threshold categorization of enhancers. TEAD motif scoring. Comparison to ENCODE/Roadmap tissue H3K27ac data. Verteporfin dose-response.

hypotheses.title: Direct LINC-Transmitted Mechanical Force Functions as a Physical Pioneer Factor, Unwinding Nucleosomes at Mechanosensitive Enhancers Independent of ATP-Dependent Remodelers

connection.bridge: LINC complex → physical nucleosome displacement at chromatin domain boundaries → transcription-independent enhancer opening

hypotheses.confidence_justification: Physics is quantitatively sound (30 pN >> 2 pN threshold at LINC level). Sun 2020 frequency dependence supports force-dependent chromatin unwinding. But force distribution across millions of nucleosomes may be insufficient per-nucleosome. Selectivity mechanism is speculative.
hypotheses.groundedness_explanation: Nucleosome unwrapping forces ~2-3 pN (optical tweezers literature). LINC force ~10-100 pN (computational validation). LAP2β force-chromatin coupling (Sun 2023). Frequency dependence (Sun 2020). Physical pioneer factor concept and stress concentration at domain boundaries are PARAMETRIC.
grounded_claims: Nucleosome unwrapping force ~2-3 pN outer wrap, 5-8 pN inner wrap (optical tweezers)
LINC force ~10-100 pN at nuclear envelope (Wang lab, computational validation)
LAP2β transmits force to chromatin domains (Sun 2023)
Force-dependent gene activation is frequency-specific (Sun Sci Adv 2020)
parametric_claims: Force creates accessible enhancer sites BEFORE transcription factor binding
Stress concentration at euchromatin-heterochromatin boundaries
Chromatin Young's modulus differential creates selectivity
30 pN distributed across chromatin network yields sufficient per-locus force
Physical pioneer concept as alternative to enzymatic chromatin opening
hypotheses.why_wrong: 30 pN total LINC force distributed across millions of nucleosomes yields <<2 pN per nucleosome. Stress concentration at domain boundaries may not compensate. Nucleosome unwrapping requires specific force geometry (along DNA axis). Chromatin viscous relaxation may dissipate stress. Flavopiridol has pleiotropic effects confounding ATAC-seq.
hypotheses.literature_gap: Addresses whether mechanical force opens enhancers DIRECTLY (physics) vs. INDIRECTLY (signaling). All existing work assumes indirect mechanisms. Provides new interpretation for Sun 2020 frequency data.
hypotheses.falsifiable_prediction: ATAC-seq after substrate stretching WITH transcription blocked (flavopiridol): subset of enhancers still open. These force-pioneer enhancers enriched at A/B compartment boundaries. SUN2 knockdown abolishes force-dependent opening.
hypotheses.test_protocol: NIH 3T3 on PDMS. Flavopiridol (1µM, 1h pre-treatment). Cyclic stretch (10% strain, 0.5 Hz, 1h). ATAC-seq: stretch+flavo vs no-stretch+flavo vs stretch+DMSO vs no-stretch+DMSO. siSUN2 perturbation. H3K27ac CUT&Tag time-course after flavopiridol washout.

claims_downgraded: H1: BRD4 phase separation threshold downgraded from GROUNDED to PARAMETRIC (cannot confidently cite author/year/journal for BRD4 specifically)
H1: EP300 kcat ~5/min downgraded to PARAMETRIC (no specific source)
H2: Lamin B1 tethering force downgraded to PARAMETRIC (no citation)
H7: Chromatin Young's modulus downgraded to PARAMETRIC (uncertain citation)
gaps_targeted: Gap #3: 3D genome topology / Hi-C under ECM stiffness (H5)
Gap #4: Phase-separated condensates at enhancers as mechanical sensors (H1)
Gap #5: LAD vs non-LAD enhancer mechanosensitivity (H2)
Gap #6: Tissue-specific ECM stiffness → tissue-specific enhancer programs (H6)
Anomaly #3: H3K27me3/H3K27ac antagonism under mechanical regulation (H3)
Novel: Force as physical pioneer factor (H7)
